message:
feat(telemetry-store): integrate persistent storage and SpeedScore lookups

Enhances the daemon's TelemetryStore to support persistent storage via
the new rch-telemetry SQLite backend, enabling historical queries and
durable metric retention across daemon restarts.

Changes:
- TelemetryStore now accepts optional TelemetryStorage on construction
- Automatic async persistence on telemetry ingestion via spawn_blocking
- Non-blocking persistence (fire-and-forget with warn on failure)
- In-memory VecDeque retained for fast recent access

New SpeedScore APIs:
- latest_speedscore(worker_id): Fetch most recent score from persistent
  storage, returns Option<SpeedScore> for graceful handling of workers
  without benchmark data

- speedscore_history(worker_id, since, limit, offset): Paginated queries
  with SpeedScoreHistoryPage response containing total count and entries

Storage integration:
- Arc<TelemetryStorage> shared reference for thread-safe access
- Spawn blocking for SQLite operations (not async-compatible)
- Graceful degradation when storage not configured (returns None/empty)

This enables the SpeedScore API endpoints to serve historical data
and supports the metrics dashboard time-series visualizations.

Part of the Telemetry Protocol implementation (bead 1aq).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-4ur","title":"Add reason field to SelectionResponse for no-worker cases","description":"## Parent Epic: Graceful Local Fallback (remote_compilation_helper-ne8)

## Task Description

Extend the SelectionResponse protocol to include a reason field that explains why no worker was assigned. This enables the hook to provide informative messaging when falling back to local execution.

## Current State

```rust
// In rch-common/src/protocol.rs (or similar)
pub struct SelectionResponse {
    pub worker: Option<WorkerConfig>,
    pub slots_reserved: u32,
    // ...
}
```

When no worker is available, `worker` is `None` but there's no indication of WHY.

## Changes Required

### 1. Update SelectionResponse
```rust
pub struct SelectionResponse {
    pub worker: Option<WorkerConfig>,
    pub slots_reserved: u32,
    pub reason: Option<SelectionReason>,  // NEW
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub enum SelectionReason {
    /// Worker assigned successfully
    Success,
    /// No workers configured in workers.toml
    NoWorkersConfigured,
    /// All workers are unreachable
    AllWorkersUnreachable,
    /// All workers have circuits open (after circuit breaker epic)
    AllCircuitsOpen,
    /// All workers are at capacity (no available slots)
    AllWorkersBusy,
    /// No workers match required tags/preferences
    NoMatchingWorkers,
    /// Internal error during selection
    SelectionError(String),
}
```

### 2. Update selection.rs
```rust
pub async fn select_worker(...) -> SelectionResponse {
    if workers.is_empty() {
        return SelectionResponse {
            worker: None,
            slots_reserved: 0,
            reason: Some(SelectionReason::NoWorkersConfigured),
        };
    }
    
    let healthy = workers.iter().filter(|w| w.is_healthy()).collect::<Vec<_>>();
    if healthy.is_empty() {
        return SelectionResponse {
            worker: None,
            slots_reserved: 0,
            reason: Some(SelectionReason::AllWorkersUnreachable),
        };
    }
    
    // ... selection logic ...
    
    if selected.is_none() {
        return SelectionResponse {
            worker: None,
            slots_reserved: 0,
            reason: Some(SelectionReason::AllWorkersBusy),
        };
    }
    
    SelectionResponse {
        worker: selected,
        slots_reserved: cores,
        reason: Some(SelectionReason::Success),
    }
}
```

### 3. Update API serialization
Ensure the new field is properly serialized in the HTTP response from daemon.

## Files to Modify
- `rch-common/src/protocol.rs` or `rch-common/src/types.rs`
- `rchd/src/selection.rs`
- `rchd/src/api.rs`

## Testing

Add tests for each SelectionReason case:
```rust
#[test]
fn test_selection_response_no_workers() {
    // Empty pool returns NoWorkersConfigured
}

#[test]
fn test_selection_response_all_unreachable() {
    // All workers Unreachable returns AllWorkersUnreachable
}

#[test]
fn test_selection_response_all_busy() {
    // All slots used returns AllWorkersBusy
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SelectionReason enum defined with all cases
- [ ] SelectionResponse includes reason field
- [ ] selection.rs populates reason correctly for each case
- [ ] API serializes reason in JSON response
- [ ] Unit tests cover all reason variants
- [ ] Existing tests updated/pass

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-52l","title":"Integrate circuit state into WorkerHealth and health check loop","description":"## Overview

Integrate circuit state into WorkerHealth and the health check loop. This ensures worker availability reflects recent failure patterns and applies open/half‑open logic consistently.

## Goals

1. Extend `WorkerHealth` to carry `CircuitState` + `CircuitStats`
2. Drive circuit transitions based on health check outcomes
3. Expose circuit state in worker status + status API
4. Ensure transitions are logged + observable

## Implementation Plan

1. Add `circuit: CircuitStats` to `WorkerHealth`
2. Update health check loop:
   - On successful health check: `record_success`
   - On failed health check: `record_failure`
3. When circuit is `Open`, mark worker as `Unreachable`/`Degraded` for selection
4. When circuit is `HalfOpen`, allow limited probes (from config)

## Edge Cases

- Worker status “Healthy” but circuit open: selection should exclude it
- Health checks continue even if circuit open (to allow recovery)

## Tests

- Unit: `WorkerHealth` updates with successive failures/successes
- Unit: circuit state transitions integrated with health logic
- Integration: simulate failing worker for N cycles -> circuit opens
- E2E: mock health checks with deterministic timing, verify state in `/status`

## Logging

- Log state transitions with worker id, prior state, reason
- Log half‑open probe usage at DEBUG

## Acceptance Criteria

- Circuit state updates on health checks
- `/status` exposes circuit state + timestamps
- Selection excludes open circuits
- Tests cover transition paths

## Dependencies

- Circuit state enum/config (remote_compilation_helper-62v)

## Blocks

- Circuit breaker integration tests (remote_compilation_helper-7nj)
- Selection integration (remote_compilation_helper-ova)

Implement Prometheus metrics export endpoint for rchd, enabling production monitoring dashboards (Grafana) and alerting infrastructure.

## Background and Justification
While the web dashboard shows real-time telemetry, production deployments need:
- Long-term metrics storage (Prometheus/VictoriaMetrics)
- Alerting on anomalies (AlertManager)
- Correlation with other infrastructure metrics
- Historical analysis beyond our SQLite retention

### Implementation with metrics crate

\`\`\`rust
use metrics::{counter, gauge, histogram, describe_counter, describe_gauge, describe_histogram};
use metrics_exporter_prometheus::PrometheusBuilder;

pub fn init_metrics(config: &MetricsConfig) -> Result<()> {
    // Register metric descriptions
    describe_counter!("rch_jobs_total", "Total jobs processed");
    describe_gauge!("rch_worker_up", "Worker availability (1=up, 0=down)");
    describe_histogram!("rch_job_duration_seconds", "Job execution duration");
    
    // Start Prometheus exporter
    PrometheusBuilder::new()
        .with_http_listener(config.listen_addr)
        .install()?;
    
    Ok(())
}

// Usage in code:
pub async fn complete_job(&self, job: &Job, result: &JobResult) {
    let status = if result.success { "completed" } else { "failed" };
    counter!("rch_jobs_total", "status" => status).increment(1);
    histogram!("rch_job_duration_seconds").record(result.duration.as_secs_f64());
}

pub fn update_worker_telemetry(&self, worker_id: &str, telemetry: &Telemetry) {
    gauge!("rch_worker_cpu_percent", "worker_id" => worker_id.to_string())
        .set(telemetry.cpu_percent);
    gauge!("rch_worker_memory_used_percent", "worker_id" => worker_id.to_string())
        .set(telemetry.memory_used_percent);
}
\`\`\`

### Endpoint Configuration

\`\`\`toml
[metrics]
enabled = true
listen_addr = "0.0.0.0:9090"
path = "/metrics"
\`\`\`

### Example Prometheus Scrape Config
\`\`\`yaml
scrape_configs:
  - job_name: 'rch-daemon'
    static_configs:
      - targets: ['rchd-host:9090']
    scrape_interval: 15s
\`\`\`

### Example Grafana Dashboard Queries

\`\`\`
# Job success rate
sum(rate(rch_jobs_total{status="completed"}[5m])) / sum(rate(rch_jobs_total[5m]))

# Average job duration
histogram_quantile(0.95, sum(rate(rch_job_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])) by (le))

# Worker availability
sum(rch_worker_up) / count(rch_worker_up)

# Queue depth alert
rch_job_queue_depth > 10
\`\`\`

### Alert Rules (AlertManager)
\`\`\`yaml
groups:
  - name: rch-alerts
    rules:
      - alert: WorkerDown
        expr: rch_worker_up == 0
        for: 5m
        labels:
          severity: warning
        annotations:
          summary: "Worker {{ \$labels.worker_id }} is down"
          
      - alert: HighJobFailureRate
        expr: rate(rch_jobs_total{status="failed"}[5m]) / rate(rch_jobs_total[5m]) > 0.1
        for: 5m
        labels:
          severity: critical
        annotations:
          summary: "Job failure rate above 10%"
          
      - alert: HighQueueDepth
        expr: rch_job_queue_depth > 20
        for: 10m
        labels:
          severity: warning
        annotations:
          summary: "Job queue depth is {{ \$value }}"
\`\`\`

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
\`\`\`rust
#[test]
fn test_metrics_registration() {
    info!("TEST START: test_metrics_registration");
    let config = MetricsConfig::default();
    info!("INPUT: Default metrics config");
    let result = init_metrics(&config);
    info!("RESULT: init_metrics returned {:?}", result.is_ok());
    assert!(result.is_ok());
    info!("TEST PASS: test_metrics_registration");
}

#[tokio::test]
async fn test_metrics_endpoint() {
    info!("TEST START: test_metrics_endpoint");
    let config = MetricsConfig { listen_addr: "127.0.0.1:0".parse().unwrap(), .. };
    init_metrics(&config).unwrap();
    
    // Record some metrics
    counter!("rch_jobs_total", "status" => "completed").increment(5);
    gauge!("rch_worker_up", "worker_id" => "test").set(1.0);
    
    // Scrape endpoint
    let resp = reqwest::get(&format!("http://{}/metrics", config.listen_addr)).await.unwrap();
    let body = resp.text().await.unwrap();
    
    info!("RESULT: Metrics response contains {} bytes", body.len());
    assert!(body.contains("rch_jobs_total"));
    assert!(body.contains("rch_worker_up"));
    info!("TEST PASS: test_metrics_endpoint");
}

 {"id":"remote_compilation_helper-5ff","title":"Structured Logging Infrastructure","description":"## Overview
Implement a consistent, structured logging framework across all RCH components (rch, rchd, rch-telemetry) with configurable verbosity, log rotation, and machine-parseable output.

## Background and Justification
Good logging is essential for:
- Debugging production issues
- Understanding test failures
- Performance profiling
- Audit trails
- User support

Current state: ad-hoc println!/eprintln! statements. Goal: structured, leveled, rotated logs.

## Implementation Details

### Log Structure
Every log message includes:
\`\`\`rust
{
    "timestamp": "2026-01-17T12:34:56.789Z",
    "level": "INFO",
    "target": "rchd::worker::selection",
    "message": "Selected worker for job",
    "span": {
        "job_id": "abc123",
        "worker_id": "css"
    },
    "fields": {
        "strategy": "balanced",
        "candidates": 4,
        "selected_score": 85.2
    }
}
\`\`\`

### Framework: tracing + tracing-subscriber

\`\`\`rust
use tracing::{info, warn, error, debug, trace, instrument, span, Level};
use tracing_subscriber::{fmt, layer::SubscriberExt, util::SubscriberInitExt, EnvFilter};

pub fn init_logging(config: &LogConfig) -> Result<()> {
    let env_filter = EnvFilter::try_from_default_env()
        .unwrap_or_else(|_| EnvFilter::new(&config.default_level));
    
    let fmt_layer = fmt::layer()
        .with_target(true)
        .with_thread_ids(true)
        .with_file(true)
        .with_line_number(true);
    
    let fmt_layer = if config.json_format {
        fmt_layer.json()
    } else {
        fmt_layer.pretty()
    };
    
    let file_layer = if let Some(path) = &config.file_path {
        let file = rolling::daily(path.parent().unwrap(), path.file_name().unwrap());
        Some(fmt::layer().with_writer(file).json())
    } else {
        None
    };
    
    tracing_subscriber::registry()
        .with(env_filter)
        .with(fmt_layer)
        .with(file_layer)
        .init();
    
    Ok(())
}
\`\`\`

### Instrumentation Patterns

#### Function-Level Tracing
\`\`\`rust
#[instrument(skip(self), fields(worker_id = %worker_id))]
pub async fn select_worker(&self, job: &Job) -> Result<WorkerId> {
    debug!("Starting worker selection");
    
    let candidates = self.get_eligible_workers(job).await?;
    trace!(candidate_count = candidates.len(), "Found eligible workers");
    
    let selected = self.apply_strategy(&candidates)?;
    info!(
        selected_worker = %selected.id,
        score = selected.speedscore.map(|s| s.total).unwrap_or(0.0),
        "Worker selected"
    );
    
    Ok(selected.id)
}
\`\`\`

#### Span Context for Related Operations
\`\`\`rust
pub async fn handle_build_request(&self, request: BuildRequest) -> Result<BuildResponse> {
    let span = span!(Level::INFO, "build_request", 
        request_id = %request.id,
        user = %request.user,
        project = %request.project,
    );
    let _enter = span.enter();
    
    info!("Received build request");
    // All subsequent logs in this scope include request context
    
    let worker = self.select_worker(&request.job).await?;
    let result = self.execute_build(worker, &request).await?;
    
    info!(
        duration_ms = result.duration.as_millis(),
        exit_code = result.exit_code,
        "Build completed"
    );
    
    Ok(result)
}
\`\`\`

#### Error Context
\`\`\`rust
pub async fn connect_worker(&self, worker_id: &str) -> Result<Connection> {
    let result = self.inner_connect(worker_id).await;
    
    if let Err(ref e) = result {
        error!(
            worker_id = %worker_id,
            error = %e,
            error_kind = ?e.kind(),
            "Failed to connect to worker"
        );
    }
    
    result
}
\`\`\`

### Log Levels Usage Guide

| Level | Use For | Example |
|-------|---------|---------|
| ERROR | Failures requiring attention | Connection failures, invalid config |
| WARN | Recoverable issues | Retry attempts, deprecation warnings |
| INFO | Significant events | Job start/complete, worker selection |
| DEBUG | Detailed flow information | Function entry/exit, intermediate values |
| TRACE | Very verbose debugging | Loop iterations, raw data |

### Progress Types

#### 1. Spinner - Unknown Duration Operations
\`\`\`
⠋ Connecting to gpu-worker...
⠙ Probing mock-worker...
⠹ Starting daemon...
\`\`\`
- Style: Braille dots `⠋⠙⠹⠸⠼⠴⠦⠧⠇⠏` (indicatif `Dots` style)
- Message updates as operation progresses
- Completes with ✓ or ✗ and final message
- Use for: SSH connection, daemon startup, single worker probe

**Implementation with tokio feature:**
\`\`\`rust
use indicatif::{ProgressBar, ProgressStyle};
use std::time::Duration;

pub async fn with_spinner<F, T>(ctx: &OutputContext, message: &str, future: F) -> T 
where
    F: std::future::Future<Output = T>,
{
    if !ctx.colors_enabled || ctx.is_json() || ctx.is_quiet() {
        return future.await;
    }
    
    let pb = ProgressBar::new_spinner();
    pb.set_style(ProgressStyle::default_spinner()
        .tick_strings(&["⠋", "⠙", "⠹", "⠸", "⠼", "⠴", "⠦", "⠧", "⠇", "⠏"])
        .template("{spinner:.cyan} {msg}")
        .unwrap());
    pb.set_message(message.to_string());
    pb.enable_steady_tick(Duration::from_millis(80));
    
    let result = future.await;
    pb.finish_and_clear();
    result
}
\`\`\`

- Use for: `workers probe --all`, `workers benchmark`

**Async MultiProgress pattern:**
\`\`\`rust
use indicatif::{MultiProgress, ProgressBar};
use futures::stream::{self, StreamExt};

pub async fn probe_all_workers(workers: &[WorkerConfig], ctx: &OutputContext) -> Vec<ProbeResult> {
    let m = MultiProgress::new();
    
    let handles: Vec<_> = workers.iter().map(|worker| {
        let pb = m.add(ProgressBar::new_spinner());
        pb.set_prefix(format!("{:12}", worker.id));
        pb.enable_steady_tick(Duration::from_millis(80));
        
        async move {
            let result = probe_worker(worker).await;
            match &result {
                Ok(_) => pb.finish_with_message("✓ OK"),
                Err(e) => pb.finish_with_message(format!("✗ {}", e)),
            }
            result
        }
    }).collect();
    
    futures::future::join_all(handles).await
}
\`\`\`

### Critical: Progress + Streaming Output Coexistence

During `execute_remote`, compilation output streams to the terminal. This conflicts with progress indicators.

**Solution: Suspend/Resume Pattern**
\`\`\`rust
let m = MultiProgress::new();
let pb = m.add(ProgressBar::new_spinner());
pb.set_message("Compiling...");

// Suspend progress drawing before streaming
m.set_draw_target(ProgressDrawTarget::hidden());

// Stream compilation output
execute_streaming(command, |line| println!("{}", line)).await?;

// Resume progress drawing
m.set_draw_target(ProgressDrawTarget::stderr());
pb.finish_with_message("✓ Compiled");
\`\`\`

### rsync Progress Integration

rsync with `--info=progress2` outputs:
\`\`\`
    123,456,789 100%   10.50MB/s    0:00:11 (xfr#42, to-chk=0/100)
\`\`\`

**Parsing approach with async streams:**
\`\`\`rust
use tokio::io::{AsyncBufReadExt, BufReader};
use tokio::process::Command;
use regex::Regex;

pub async fn rsync_with_progress(
    args: &[&str], 
    pb: &ProgressBar
) -> Result<SyncResult> {
    let mut cmd = Command::new("rsync");
    cmd.args(args)
        .arg("--info=progress2")
        .stdout(Stdio::piped())
        .stderr(Stdio::piped());
    
    let mut child = cmd.spawn()?;
    let stdout = child.stdout.take().unwrap();
    let mut reader = BufReader::new(stdout).lines();
    
    let progress_re = Regex::new(r"(\d+)\s+(\d+)%").unwrap();
    
    while let Some(line) = reader.next_line().await? {
        if let Some(caps) = progress_re.captures(&line) {
            let bytes: u64 = caps[1].replace(",", "").parse().unwrap_or(0);
            pb.set_position(bytes);
        }
    }
    
    let status = child.wait().await?;
    Ok(SyncResult { success: status.success(), .. })
}

### Cancellation Handling (Ctrl+C)

Use tokio's signal handling:
\`\`\`rust
use tokio::signal;
use tokio::select;

pub async fn with_cancellation<F, T>(pb: &ProgressBar, future: F) -> Result<T>
where
    F: std::future::Future<Output = Result<T>>,
{
    select! {
        result = future => result,
        _ = signal::ctrl_c() => {
            pb.abandon_with_message("Cancelled");
            anyhow::bail!("Operation cancelled by user")
        }
    }
}
\`\`\`

### Performance Considerations
- Update progress at most 10x/second (100ms debounce)
- Don't update on every byte - batch updates
- Spinner tick rate: 80ms (12.5 fps) - smooth without CPU waste
- Use `enable_steady_tick()` for automatic animation

### Progress Module API
\`\`\`rust
// rch/src/ui/progress.rs

use indicatif::{MultiProgress, ProgressBar, ProgressStyle, ProgressDrawTarget};
use std::time::Duration;

/// Spinner for unknown-duration operations
pub struct Spinner {
    inner: ProgressBar,
    ctx: OutputContext,
}

impl Spinner {
    pub fn new(ctx: &OutputContext, message: &str) -> Self {
        if !should_show_progress(ctx) {
            return Self { inner: ProgressBar::hidden(), ctx: ctx.clone() };
        }
        
        let pb = ProgressBar::new_spinner();
        pb.set_style(ProgressStyle::default_spinner()
            .tick_strings(&["⠋", "⠙", "⠹", "⠸", "⠼", "⠴", "⠦", "⠧", "⠇", "⠏"])
            .template("{spinner:.cyan} {msg}")
            .unwrap());
        pb.set_message(message.to_string());
        pb.enable_steady_tick(Duration::from_millis(80));
        
        Self { inner: pb, ctx: ctx.clone() }
    }
    
    pub fn set_message(&self, msg: &str) {
        self.inner.set_message(msg.to_string());
    }
    
    pub fn finish_success(&self, msg: &str) {
        self.inner.finish_with_message(format!("✓ {}", msg));
    }
    
    pub fn finish_error(&self, msg: &str) {
        self.inner.finish_with_message(format!("✗ {}", msg));
    }
    
    pub fn finish_warning(&self, msg: &str) {
        self.inner.finish_with_message(format!("⚠ {}", msg));
    }
}

/// Progress bar for known-size operations
pub struct TransferProgress {
    inner: ProgressBar,
}

impl TransferProgress {
    pub fn new(ctx: &OutputContext, total: u64, label: &str) -> Self {
        if !should_show_progress(ctx) {
            return Self { inner: ProgressBar::hidden() };
        }
        
        let pb = ProgressBar::new(total);
        pb.set_style(ProgressStyle::default_bar()
            .template("{msg:12} [{bar:40.cyan/blue}] {bytes}/{total_bytes} {bytes_per_sec} ETA {eta}")
            .unwrap()
            .progress_chars("█▓░"));
        pb.set_message(label.to_string());
        
        Self { inner: pb }
    }
    
    pub fn set_position(&self, pos: u64) {
        self.inner.set_position(pos);
    }
    
    pub fn finish(&self) {
        self.inner.finish_and_clear();
    }
}

/// Step progress for multi-phase operations
pub struct StepProgress {
    steps: Vec<String>,
    current: usize,
    ctx: OutputContext,
}

impl StepProgress {
    pub fn new(ctx: &OutputContext, steps: &[&str]) -> Self {
        Self {
            steps: steps.iter().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect(),
            current: 0,
            ctx: ctx.clone(),
        }
    }
    
    pub fn start_step(&mut self, idx: usize) {
        self.current = idx;
        self.print_steps();
    }
    
    pub fn complete_step(&mut self, idx: usize, message: &str) {
        // Mark step complete with message
    }
    
    fn print_steps(&self) {
        // Print step indicators with ✓ ◐ ○
    }
}

/// Multi-progress manager for parallel operations
pub struct MultiProgressManager {
    multi: MultiProgress,
    ctx: OutputContext,
}

impl MultiProgressManager {
    pub fn new(ctx: &OutputContext) -> Self {
        let multi = if should_show_progress(ctx) {
            MultiProgress::new()
        } else {
            MultiProgress::with_draw_target(ProgressDrawTarget::hidden())
        };
        Self { multi, ctx: ctx.clone() }
    }
    
    pub fn add_spinner(&self, prefix: &str, message: &str) -> Spinner {
        let pb = self.multi.add(ProgressBar::new_spinner());
        pb.set_prefix(prefix.to_string());
        pb.set_message(message.to_string());
        pb.enable_steady_tick(Duration::from_millis(80));
        Spinner { inner: pb, ctx: self.ctx.clone() }
    }
    
    pub fn suspend<F, T>(&self, f: F) -> T
    where
        F: FnOnce() -> T,
    {
        self.multi.suspend(f)
    }
}

    # Test spinner appears during probe

    RCH_MOCK_SSH=1 "$RCH" workers probe mock-worker 2>"$stderr_file"

    log "INFO" "PROGRESS" "Pipe detection OK"

    # Test progress completes without hanging
    if ! timeout 10 bash -c 'RCH_MOCK_SSH=1 '"$RCH"' workers probe --all 2>&1' > /dev/null; then
        log "FAIL" "PROGRESS" "Progress indicators caused hang"

 ### Grafana Dashboard
 
-Import the provided Grafana dashboard or create your own:
-
-Key panels:
-1. **Build throughput** - Builds per minute by worker
-2. **Build duration** - P50, P95, P99 latency
-3. **Worker health** - Status heatmap
-4. **Slot utilization** - Used vs available slots
-5. **Transfer rates** - MB/s to workers
-6. **Circuit breaker events** - Timeline of state changes
+Import the provided Grafana dashboard from `docs/observability/grafana-dashboard.json`:
+
+```bash
+# Import via Grafana UI:
+# 1. Go to Dashboards > Import
+# 2. Upload the JSON file or paste its contents
+# 3. Select your Prometheus data source
+```
+
+Key panels included:
+1. **Overview** - Healthy workers, active builds, queue depth, slot utilization
+2. **Build Metrics** - Throughput by result, duration percentiles
+3. **Worker Metrics** - Slots per worker, health check latency, status
+4. **Decision Latency (SLA Critical)** - P95/P99 for non-compilation (<1ms) and compilation (<5ms)
+5. **Circuit Breaker** - State per worker, trip/recovery events
+6. **Transfer Metrics** - Upload/download throughput and duration
+7. **Classification Tiers** - Distribution and latency by tier
+8. **API Metrics** - Request rate by endpoint, active connections
+
+### Prometheus AlertManager Rules
+
+Deploy the alert rules from `docs/observability/prometheus-alerts.yaml`:
+
+```bash
+# Add to your Prometheus alerting rules configuration:
+cp docs/observability/prometheus-alerts.yaml /etc/prometheus/rules/rch-alerts.yaml
+
+# Or include via prometheus.yml:
+# rule_files:
+#   - "rules/rch-alerts.yaml"
+```
+
+Alert groups:
+- **rch-worker-health** - Worker offline, stale, degraded alerts
+- **rch-circuit-breaker** - Circuit breaker open, high trip rate
+- **rch-build-health** - Failure rate, queue depth alerts
+- **rch-decision-latency-sla** - CRITICAL alerts for latency SLA breaches
+- **rch-transfer** - Slow transfer warnings
+- **rch-daemon** - Daemon down, high connection count
+- **rch-slot-utilization** - Capacity planning alerts

+# RCH Prometheus AlertManager Rules
+#
+# Deploy to your Prometheus server as part of the alerting rules configuration.
+# These rules are designed to catch issues before they impact developer productivity.
+#
+# Severity levels:
+#   - critical: Immediate paging, likely impacting all users
+#   - warning: Should be investigated within hours
+#   - info: For awareness, may self-resolve
+#
+# Decision latency budgets (from AGENTS.md):
+#   - Non-compilation commands: < 1ms (P95)
+#   - Compilation commands: < 5ms (P95)
+
+groups:
+  - name: rch-worker-health
+    interval: 30s
+    rules:
+      # CRITICAL: All workers offline means compilation offloading is completely unavailable
+      - alert: RchAllWorkersDown
+        expr: sum(rch_worker_status{status="current"} == 1) == 0
+        for: 2m
+        labels:
+          severity: critical
+        annotations:
+          summary: "All RCH workers are offline"
+          description: "No healthy workers available for remote compilation. All builds will fall back to local execution."
+          runbook_url: "https://docs.rch.dev/runbooks/all-workers-down"
+
+      # WARNING: Individual worker offline
+      - alert: RchWorkerDown
+        expr: rch_worker_status{status="current"} == 0
+        for: 5m
+        labels:
+          severity: warning
+        annotations:
+          summary: "RCH worker {{ $labels.worker }} is offline"
+          description: "Worker {{ $labels.worker }} has been unreachable for more than 5 minutes."
+          runbook_url: "https://docs.rch.dev/runbooks/worker-recovery"
+
+      # WARNING: Worker not seen recently (stale health check)
+      - alert: RchWorkerStale
+        expr: time() - rch_worker_last_seen_timestamp > 300
+        for: 5m
+        labels:
+          severity: warning
+        annotations:
+          summary: "RCH worker {{ $labels.worker }} health check stale"
+          description: "Worker {{ $labels.worker }} hasn't reported healthy in over 5 minutes."
+
+      # INFO: Worker degraded but still functional
+      - alert: RchWorkerDegraded
+        expr: rch_worker_status{status="current"} == 2
+        for: 10m
+        labels:
+          severity: info
+        annotations:
+          summary: "RCH worker {{ $labels.worker }} is degraded"
+          description: "Worker {{ $labels.worker }} is experiencing issues but still accepting builds."
+
+  - name: rch-circuit-breaker
+    interval: 30s
+    rules:
+      # WARNING: Circuit breaker open means worker is being avoided
+      - alert: RchCircuitBreakerOpen
+        expr: rch_circuit_state == 2
+        for: 5m
+        labels:
+          severity: warning
+        annotations:
+          summary: "Circuit breaker open for worker {{ $labels.worker }}"
+          description: "Worker {{ $labels.worker }} circuit breaker is open due to repeated failures. Traffic is being routed away."
+          runbook_url: "https://docs.rch.dev/runbooks/circuit-breaker"
+
+      # WARNING: High circuit trip rate indicates systemic issues
+      - alert: RchHighCircuitTripRate
+        expr: sum(increase(rch_circuit_trips_total[1h])) > 10
+        for: 5m
+        labels:
+          severity: warning
+        annotations:
+          summary: "High circuit breaker trip rate"
+          description: "More than 10 circuit breaker trips in the last hour. Check worker stability."
+
+  - name: rch-build-health
+    interval: 30s
+    rules:
+      # WARNING: High build failure rate
+      - alert: RchHighBuildFailureRate
+        expr: |
+          sum(rate(rch_builds_total{result="failure"}[5m])) /
+          sum(rate(rch_builds_total[5m])) > 0.1
+        for: 5m
+        labels:
+          severity: warning
+        annotations:
+          summary: "High RCH build failure rate ({{ $value | humanizePercentage }})"
+          description: "More than 10% of builds are failing. Investigate worker health and network connectivity."
+
+      # CRITICAL: Very high failure rate
+      - alert: RchCriticalBuildFailureRate
+        expr: |
+          sum(rate(rch_builds_total{result="failure"}[5m])) /
+          sum(rate(rch_builds_total[5m])) > 0.25
+        for: 2m
+        labels:
+          severity: critical
+        annotations:
+          summary: "Critical RCH build failure rate ({{ $value | humanizePercentage }})"
+          description: "More than 25% of builds are failing. Immediate investigation required."
+
+      # WARNING: Build queue backing up
+      - alert: RchHighQueueDepth
+        expr: rch_build_queue_depth > 10
+        for: 10m
+        labels:
+          severity: warning
+        annotations:
+          summary: "RCH build queue depth is {{ $value }}"
+          description: "Builds are queuing up. Consider adding workers or investigating slow builds."
+
+      # CRITICAL: Severe queue backlog
+      - alert: RchCriticalQueueDepth
+        expr: rch_build_queue_depth > 50
+        for: 5m
+        labels:
+          severity: critical
+        annotations:
+          summary: "Critical RCH build queue depth ({{ $value }} pending)"
+          description: "Build queue is severely backed up. Developer productivity is impacted."
+
+  - name: rch-decision-latency-sla
+    interval: 15s
+    rules:
+      # CRITICAL: Non-compilation decision latency SLA breach (must be < 1ms P95)
+      - alert: RchNonCompilationLatencySlaBreak
+        expr: |
+          histogram_quantile(0.95,
+            sum(rate(rch_decision_latency_seconds_bucket{decision_type="non_compilation"}[5m])) by (le)
+          ) > 0.001
+        for: 5m
+        labels:
+          severity: critical
+        annotations:
+          summary: "Non-compilation decision latency SLA breach (P95: {{ $value | humanizeDuration }})"
+          description: |
+            Non-compilation commands are taking longer than 1ms at P95.
+            This violates the AGENTS.md latency SLA and may cause noticeable shell lag.
+          runbook_url: "https://docs.rch.dev/runbooks/decision-latency"
+
+      # CRITICAL: Compilation decision latency SLA breach (must be < 5ms P95)
+      - alert: RchCompilationLatencySlaBreak
+        expr: |
+          histogram_quantile(0.95,
+            sum(rate(rch_decision_latency_seconds_bucket{decision_type="compilation"}[5m])) by (le)
+          ) > 0.005
+        for: 5m
+        labels:
+          severity: critical
+        annotations:
+          summary: "Compilation decision latency SLA breach (P95: {{ $value | humanizeDuration }})"
+          description: |
+            Compilation decision latency exceeds 5ms at P95.
+            This violates the AGENTS.md latency SLA.
+          runbook_url: "https://docs.rch.dev/runbooks/decision-latency"
+
+      # WARNING: Budget violations detected
+      - alert: RchDecisionBudgetViolations
+        expr: increase(rch_decision_budget_violations_total[1h]) > 100
+        for: 5m
+        labels:
+          severity: warning
+        annotations:
+          summary: "High rate of decision budget violations ({{ $value }} in last hour)"
+          description: "Many decisions are exceeding their latency budget. Performance may be degrading."
+
+  - name: rch-transfer
+    interval: 30s
+    rules:
+      # WARNING: Slow transfers indicate network issues
+      - alert: RchSlowTransfers
+        expr: |
+          histogram_quantile(0.95,
+            sum(rate(rch_transfer_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])) by (direction, le)
+          ) > 30
+        for: 5m
+        labels:
+          severity: warning
+        annotations:
+          summary: "Slow RCH transfers ({{ $labels.direction }} P95: {{ $value | humanizeDuration }})"
+          description: "File transfers are taking longer than expected. Check network connectivity to workers."
+
+      # WARNING: Very low compression ratio may indicate issues
+      - alert: RchPoorCompression
+        expr: |
+          histogram_quantile(0.50,
+            sum(rate(rch_transfer_compression_ratio_bucket[1h])) by (le)
+          ) > 0.9
+        for: 30m
+        labels:
+          severity: info
+        annotations:
+          summary: "Poor compression ratio for transfers"
+          description: "Compression is not providing significant benefit. This may be expected for already-compressed artifacts."
+
+  - name: rch-daemon
+    interval: 30s
+    rules:
+      # CRITICAL: Daemon process down (detected by absent metric)
+      - alert: RchDaemonDown
+        expr: absent(rch_daemon_uptime_seconds) == 1
+        for: 1m
+        labels:
+          severity: critical
+        annotations:
+          summary: "RCH daemon is not running"
+          description: "The rchd daemon is not exposing metrics. It may have crashed or failed to start."
+          runbook_url: "https://docs.rch.dev/runbooks/daemon-recovery"
+
+      # WARNING: High connection count may indicate client issues
+      - alert: RchHighConnectionCount
+        expr: rch_daemon_connections_active > 100
+        for: 5m
+        labels:
+          severity: warning
+        annotations:
+          summary: "High RCH daemon connection count ({{ $value }})"
+          description: "Unusually high number of active connections. Check for connection leaks or runaway clients."
+
+  - name: rch-slot-utilization
+    interval: 30s
+    rules:
+      # WARNING: High slot utilization means builds may queue
+      - alert: RchHighSlotUtilization
+        expr: |
+          1 - (sum(rch_worker_slots_available) / sum(rch_worker_slots_total)) > 0.9
+        for: 15m
+        labels:
+          severity: warning
+        annotations:
+          summary: "High RCH slot utilization ({{ $value | humanizePercentage }})"
+          description: "Over 90% of build slots are in use. Consider adding workers to maintain capacity headroom."
+
+      # INFO: Very low utilization may indicate over-provisioning
+      - alert: RchLowSlotUtilization
+        expr: |
+          1 - (sum(rch_worker_slots_available) / sum(rch_worker_slots_total)) < 0.1
+        for: 1h
+        labels:
+          severity: info
+        annotations:
+          summary: "Low RCH slot utilization ({{ $value | humanizePercentage }})"
+          description: "Slot utilization is below 10%. You may be able to reduce worker count to save costs."
